title: tragoselinum saxifragum
intro: Tragoselinum saxifragum, also known as Sium saxatile, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative effects. The health benefits of Tragoselinum saxifragum include relief from stomach cramps, bloating, and gas, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
